title: Today's AI Brief: Cisco and OpenAI redefine enterprise engineering with Codex
summary: Cisco and OpenAI team up to redefine enterprise engineering with Codex, while Pope Leo XIV's encyclical touches on AI's power.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI brief. I've got a couple of interesting stories to share with you. First up, Cisco and OpenAI are joining forces to shake up enterprise engineering with Codex. This collaboration aims to help Cisco scale AI-native development, speed up AI defense work, and automate defect remediation. My read is that this partnership could significantly impact how companies approach AI integration and management.

So, what does this mean? Essentially, Codex is being used to streamline and enhance Cisco's engineering processes. I imagine it's like having a super-smart assistant that helps you debug and improve your code. This could be a game-changer for companies looking to up their AI game. Plus, it shows how AI is becoming more mainstream in the enterprise world.

Also making waves is Pope Leo XIV's first encyclical, 'Magnifica Humanitas.' The document touches on the power of AI and its influence on our daily lives. It's interesting to see religious leaders weighing in on the implications of AI – it's a reminder that this tech isn't just about code and machines, but about how it affects us as humans. And who knows, maybe we'll see more spiritual takes on AI in the future.

Cisco and OpenAI redefine enterprise engineering with Codex is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Cisco and OpenAI are redefining enterprise engineering with Codex, helping Cisco scale AI-native development, accelerate AI Defense work, and automate defect remediation. The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

What Pope Leo XIV’s First Encyclical Says About the Power of AI is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: An algorithm decides what we see, another filters what we read, and still others enter into the processes that govern work, information, and collective choices. In the encyclical Magnifica Humanitas . the first signed by Pope Leo XIV and published on May 25, artificial intelligence is not viewed as just another technology; it is part of the invisible infras... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
So that is the brief version: a few headlines, a little context, and just enough connective tissue to make the day easier to follow without turning the whole thing into homework.
